_doc: LEAK-SAFE methodology talking points for the P2 content-wire (Tier 1, Ogie msg 8078). The consumer draws ONLY from this curated pool — it NEVER reads hunt-file specifics. Every entry is generalized methodology (lens/doctrine/anchor-class). Operator-editable: add an entry when a NEGATE produces a publishable, target-stripped methodology win. RULE: no live-target name, no contract/function/address, no platform+scope combo. When in doubt, generalize harder.

arsenal_state.headline: Our Clarity security arsenal now spans 4 protocol-classes — a compounding library of reusable audit lenses, not more LLM tokens.

lenses.title: ERC4626-share-vs-separate-index pricing-consistency
lenses.tweet: A vault share used as collateral but priced by a SEPARATE liquidity index — while the vault redeems via ERC4626 shares — must track that index. If it over-states the share: over-borrow → bad debt.
lenses.claim: When a tokenized-vault share is used as collateral but priced by a SEPARATE liquidity index (aToken-style) while the vault redeems via ERC4626 total-assets/total-supply, the two valuations must track — or the gap becomes over-borrow and bad debt. The dangerous direction is the index OVER-stating share value. Check the treasury-dilution formula, the rounding direction, and whether the utilization denominator equals total-assets.

lenses.title: Cross-protocol exchange-rate-source manipulation
lenses.tweet: Protocol B reads protocol A's internal rate as an oracle (LST ratio, vault price, AMM TWAP). Safe only if: A's rate isn't in-tx movable, OR the move is unrecoverable, OR B bounds it. Movable + recoverable + unbounded = cross-protocol critical.
lenses.claim: When protocol B consumes protocol A's INTERNAL exchange-rate as an oracle (an LST ratio, a vault share-price, an AMM TWAP), three questions decide safety: (1) is A's rate in-tx movable — raw-balance/donation vs a tracked accounting variable? (2) is the move RECOVERABLE in-tx, or only a permanent donation that dilutes across all holders? (3) does B's consuming math BOUND the rate? Movable + recoverable + unbounded = a cross-protocol critical; movable-but-unrecoverable-or-bounded is a fragility to monitor.

lenses.title: Tracked-var vs raw-balance accounting
lenses.tweet: A share/rate from a TRACKED accounting var is immune to donation-inflation; from a raw token balance it's only seed-state-safe. But even a partly-raw total is safe if the raw part is attacker-unrecoverable AND the consumer is bounded. Foreclosure = capture<cost + unrecoverable.
lenses.claim: A share or rate computed from a TRACKED accounting variable is structurally immune to donation-inflation; one computed from a raw token balance is only seed-state-safe. But even a partially-raw total can be safe — if the raw component is attacker-unrecoverable and the consumer is bounded. The real foreclosure is 'capture < cost AND unrecoverable', not simply 'use a tracked var'.

lenses.title: Mandatory-path arithmetic edge -> DoS / freeze / halt
lenses.tweet: Unguarded arithmetic (div-by-zero, underflow, a zero-guard testing the wrong quantity) on a MANDATORY accounting/charging path → it aborts → every entry point that must run it bricks. Deposits, withdrawals, even chain liveness. Guard the divisor itself, or cross-multiply.
lenses.claim: Generalized detector class: an unguarded arithmetic edge — divide-by-zero, subtraction underflow, or a zero-guard that tests the wrong quantity (a sum or sibling rather than the actual divisor) — sitting on a MANDATORY accounting/charging/metering path. When it aborts, every entry point that must run that calc bricks: deposits, withdrawals, liquidations, even chain liveness. The fix: guard the divisor itself, or cross-multiply to avoid the division. Real-world anchors span multiple chains and a mainnet halt.

lenses.title: The 3-guard liquidation checklist
lenses.tweet: Three guards foreclose the over-liquidation / flash-borrow-self-liquidate family: (1) a param invariant capping seizure ≤100% of collateral, (2) a post-liquidation health-buffer assert, (3) a same-block-borrow guard. Run them as a checklist — a missing one is the candidate.
lenses.claim: Three guards foreclose the over-liquidation / flash-borrow-self-liquidate family: a parameter invariant capping seizure at <=100% of collateral value, a post-liquidation health-buffer assertion, and a same-block-borrow guard. Run them as a checklist on any lending target — a missing one is the candidate.

thesis_lines: Honest calibration scales; LLM throughput doesn't. Every defense we file pre-pays the next audit.
A clean NEGATE is a product: each foreclosed surface compounds into a reusable lens that filters the next target faster.
We don't ship false positives. The discipline behind the scoring engine is pre-paid pattern recognition, not retroactive triage.
